mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate has declined from its recent peak and inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, real-rate pressure rises and equity multiples compress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average by at least 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income expectations and lower earnings outlooks, which depresses equity prices over a quarter. The effect should be visible when unemployment is trending up rather than merely flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are positive in a majority of unemployment-rise episodes, or if the signal fails to outperform unconditional returns in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding labor income and supports consumer spending, which improves near-term corporate revenue expectations and raises S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by at least 150k over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if strong payroll-growth episodes do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is unchanged or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates strengthening final demand and housing-linked activity, which signals better revenue momentum for cyclical equities and supports positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint-strength episodes in retail sales and housing starts do not outperform single-variable signals, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive after the interaction activates.

mechanism_narrative: A firm rise in INDPRO combined with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies real output growth without inflation pressure, supporting margin stability and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises more than 0.4% over 2 months while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation stays below 2% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if high-industrial-production, low-inflation episodes do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns or if inflation dominates the interaction and flips the sign.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and sustained, the market should discount stronger aggregate earnings and support S&P 500 gains over the following quarter. The signal fails if GDP growth is revised away or if growth coincides with tightening financial conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not correspond to above-median 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests, or if revision-adjusted results turn negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in RSAFS after a prior slowdown signals demand reacceleration and inventory absorption, which should lift cyclical revenue expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is strongest immediately after the acceleration begins.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth turns positive after being negative in the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if demand-reacceleration triggers do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal decays entirely after controlling for broader market trend.
